intro: Pyrus scepusiensis, also known as Scepusian pear, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus scepusiensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
